goal: Decide whether reports and operations should adopt second-level menus and produce a repo-grounded remediation plan.
deliverable: A navigation decision for overloaded views
A global rule for when to introduce secondary navigation
A phased remediation plan for reports and operations
constraints: Do not change product code in this turn
Keep the primary sidebar stable unless evidence shows it is the root problem
Stay within the current static Vue architecture
Preserve the current functional scope while restructuring later
acceptance_criteria: The requirement states a clear yes or no on second-level menus
The plan separates primary navigation, contextual submenus, and in-page actions
The target subviews for reports and operations are named concretely
product_acceptance_criteria: The next UI restructuring round can begin from a frozen IA decision instead of another debate
Future views can reuse the same submenu rule instead of inventing one-off tabs
manual_spot_checks: Confirm that the current sidebar still feels understandable at the first level
Confirm that reports and operations each bundle more than one dominant job story today
Confirm that baseline validation still passes after this planning-only round
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after the decision, phase plan, and runtime receipts are written and the baseline validation command passes.
delivery_truth_contract: State only the planning artifacts and validation actually produced in this run.
non_goals: Expanding the product into a broader ERP taxonomy
inferred_assumptions: The user wants a concrete product decision, not a neutral pros-and-cons list.
A shared submenu pattern is more useful than one custom solution for each page.
Operations and reports are the immediate pressure points, while other views can remain single-level for now.
